entry.title: Enterprise AI in Action: SAP Joule Delivers Where Others Don’t
entry.content:
AI is becoming part of everyday enterprise work, from drafting emails to designing complex processes. While ChatGPT and Gemini can generate functional specs and ABAP code, the real question is: “Why use SAP Joule?”
The difference is context – Joule is embedded in S/4HANA, understanding system configuration, business rules, workflows, and live data to deliver outputs that are accurate, compliant, and ready for real-world use. Unlike general-purpose AI, it doesn’t just produce well-written text; it grasps the technical and business landscape to ensure every recommendation aligns with your enterprise environment.
To understand this difference in practical terms, let’s look at two common S/4HANA scenarios: a Purchase Order compliance check and a Sales Order credit validation. On the surface, both appear straightforward – define the requirement, identify the enhancement spot, document the logic, and generate the necessary ABAP code.
A general-purpose AI tool can certainly help structure the functional specification and outline the technical approach. However, the real challenge in enterprise projects is not drafting the document – it is ensuring that the design aligns with the system’s actual configuration, enhancement framework, workflow setup, authorization model, and business data. This is where the distinction between generic AI and embedded, SAP-native intelligence becomes clear.

1. Purchase Order Compliance Check
Business Requirement: Block Purchase Order creation if:
• Vendor is not approved in a custom Z-table
• PO value exceeds 1,000,000
• Purchasing group = “A01”
• Mandatory attachment is missing
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Enhancement via BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST
• Logic to check vendor approval, PO value, purchasing group, and attachment
• Error messages and test cases
Pros: Clear FS structure, general logic explained
Cons: Generic — it won’t know your actual Z-table structure, workflow configuration, or document type setup. You must feed these details manually.
Using SAP Joule:
• Identifies the correct BAdI or workflow in your S/4 system
• Pulls actual vendor master and custom table fields
• Checks your approval strategy and document type configuration
• Generates FS logic aligned with your live system setup
Pros: System-grounded, accurate, reduces rework, supports compliance

2. Sales Order Credit Validation
Business Requirement: Prevent Sales Order creation if a customer’s credit limit is exceeded.
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Identify a relevant BAdI or user exit
• Check credit exposure against the limit
• Trigger an error message if the limit is exceeded
• Suggest test cases for validation
Pros: Clean structure, well-written, and easy to understand
Cons: Generic — it doesn’t know your actual credit control area, company code setup, or the exact fields and BAdIs in your S/4HANA system. All system-specific details must be manually added.
Using SAP Joule:
• Detect the active BAdI or enhancement spot in your system
• Pull the correct credit control configuration and field names
• Align logic with company code settings and authorization roles
• Suggest tests based on your live system data
Pros: Context-aware, system-aligned, lower risk of errors

Key Differentiators

  1. Verified SAP Knowledge Base: Responses are grounded in SAP's official documentation and verified sources.
  2. SAP-Specific Methodology: Understands SAP implementation approaches, configuration requirements, and technical constraints.
  3. Industry-Specific Compliance: Aligned with SAP's consulting standards and best practices.
  4. SAP Ecosystem Integration: Deep understanding of how SAP modules, technologies, and processes interconnect.

Comparison Table

Capability

SAP Joule for Consultants

ChatGPT

Gemini

Notes

SAP Domain Expertise

Deep, verified SAP knowledge from official sources

General SAP knowledge from public training data

General SAP knowledge with real-time search

Joule is trained on SAP-verified documentation

Template Generation

SAP-specific templates and methodologies

Generic business templates

Generic business templates with Google integration

Templates follow SAP best practices and standards

Functional Specification Writing

SAP implementation-focused with proper technical structure

General functional spec approaches

Structured but generic approaches

Joule understands SAP-specific requirements and constraints

Industry Standards Compliance

SAP best practices and implementation standards

General industry standards

General standards with research capabilities

Aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies

Technical Documentation Structure

SAP-aligned formats (ABAP, configuration paths, transaction codes)

Generic technical documentation

Structured technical docs

Joule provide SAP-specific technical details and paths

Integration Knowledge

Deep SAP ecosystem and module integration expertise

Broad but surface-level SAP integration knowledge

General integration knowledge

Joule understand SAP module interdependencies and best practices

Business Process Understanding

SAP module-specific processes and workflows

General business process knowledge

General business knowledge with research

Joule trained on SAP business process documentation

For functional specification writing in SAP contexts, Joule is the superior choice because:

  • Accuracy: knowledge comes from verified SAP sources rather than general internet training data. 
  • Relevance: understands SAP-specific technical requirements, configuration paths, and implementation constraints. 
  • Structure: can provide proper SAP documentation formats, including transaction codes, customization paths, and technical specifications. 
  • Best Practices: aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies and implementation standards.

We’ve seen how generic AI can draft specs and code, but context matters. In your S/4HANA projects, where has AI helped — and where has it fallen short? Share your experiences, challenges, or tips below!

Source:

SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help

Joule

Other Blogs:

From Conversational AI to Business AI: Why SAP Jou... - SAP Community

System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community

entry.title: Why Generative UI is Redefining the Future of Enterprise Software
entry.content:
The world of user interfaces is evolving rapidly, spurred by the rise of consumer AI and shifting expectations for business software. Employees want the same intuitive and conversational experiences they enjoy at home to be reflected in their workplace tools. While text-based interactions are flexible and user-friendly, they struggle with structured data. Graphical user interfaces (GUIs) excel in presenting structured information, but they are rigid and costly to customize.

Enter generative UI: the game-changing solution bridging text and GUI. Generative UI allows software interfaces to dynamically adapt to each user’s intent, context, and task. By leveraging SAP Joule and Joule Agents, these interfaces materialize on demand, grounded in real-time, accurate data, while enabling seamless execution of complex workflows.

Generative UI is the future of enterprise software. It’s not just about dashboards—it’s about interfaces that empower users to make decisions and drive action with precision and confidence.

Read the full blog to learn how generative UI is redefining business applications.

entry.title: Troubleshooting Ariba-Joule Integration errors
entry.content:
Introduction:🚀 Cracking the SAP Ariba–Joule Integration common errors:

Over the past few months, I’ve seen a common pattern across multiple customer implementations: brilliant landscapes struggling with Navigation Flow glitches, incomplete Transactional prompts, or inconsistent User Sync—all caused by a handful of tiny but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone.

To help accelerate troubleshooting and avoid post‑go‑live surprises, I’ve put together a practical breakdown of the most recurring issues and the exact fixes that have consistently stabilized the integration.

If you're enabling Joule for Ariba or supporting customers on their journey, this article will save you hours of guesswork. 🔧💡

Common issues with Navigation flow and resolution: 

Scenario 1:Navigational prompts intermittently return data

Sample prompts: Go my approvals, my task, create engagement request, created sourcing project, open supplier profile etc   

Cause: This could happen, if user has been assigned more than 150 groups in Ariba and those groups are being replicated to IAS and then to Build work zone.  To verify if the issue is due to roles replicated to BWZ,  login to BWZ and user will get error “HTTP ERROR 431”. 

Resolution: Replicate only those standard groups to BWZ which are required for Ariba-Joule integration. You can achieve this by adding a filter parameter ariba.applications.group.filter in the IPS source system (the source system configured to fetch users and groups from Ariba https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-integration-with-sap-ariba-setup-guide/ba-p/14240003 ). Using this filter ensures that only the required groups—and users assigned to those groups—are synchronized from Ariba to IAS and subsequently to BWZ.

Sample value: (displayName eq "Sourcing Agent" or displayName eq "Sourcing Approver")

Important Notes

  • Navigational flow works only with standard groups.
  • If customers assign only custom roles to users in Ariba, they must still replicate the required standard groups from Ariba → IAS → BWZ.
  • Custom groups are not supported, SAP is considering for future release 
  • This filtering process does not impact customer‑specific role assignments:
    • Customers may continue using their custom groups assignment in Ariba as usual
    • Since custom groups map back to standard roles, any Joule‑relevant standard roles tied to custom roles will still function properly through this filtered sync.
  • Recommendation:
    • Maintain a list of standard groups required for Joule navigation.
    • Additionally replicate customer‑specific custom groups only to ensure that all Ariba users sync correctly to CIS

Steps to maintain the parameter:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision
  • Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba
  • Edit and maintain the parameter and run the IPS job as described previously
List of standard roles which are required for Ariba joule Navigational scenario:

Supplier Registration Manager

Supplier Manager

Supplier Qualification Manager

Supplier Risk ESG User

Purchasing Administrator

Supplier Risk Engagement Requestor

Financial Processing Analyst

Supplier Risk Evaluation Expert

AuditBuyer

Supplier Risk Provider Registration Expert

Purchasing Agent

Joule Supplier Profile User

Invoice Manager

Sourcing Project Administrator

Purchasing Manager

Sourcing Agent

Report Manager

Limited Event Administrator

Receiving Manager

Junior Sourcing Agent

Manual Order Processor

SM Questionnaire Manager

Tax Manager

Supplier Risk Engagement Governance Analyst

Purchasing User

Supplier Qualification Request Manager

Executive Approver

Supplier Risk User

Sourcing Approver

Supplier Risk Report Expert

Category Buyer

Supplier Risk Engagement Expert

Supplier Risk Engagement Analyst

Sourcing Manager

Operational Junior Sourcing Agent

Supplier Risk Manager

Supplier Risk Configuration Expert

Supplier Request Manager

SM Process Manager

Event Administrator

Scenario 2:  Error “The application cannot be opened”

Resolution: Create a destination under the sub-account which has been created for joule. Make sure destination name is “NavigationService” follow the below article for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/configure-destination-to-use-navigation-service?locale=en-US&q=+NavigationService

Steps:

  • Login to Joule Sub-account
  • Click connectivity à Destination
  • Click create

Scenario 3: Error: You need different permissions to perform this action or I am having trouble processing your request.

Cause : This happen when user doesn’t have the required access in Ariba or if they have the required access, joule related standard roles are not assigned to user in BWZ after replication.  

To verify the roles in BWZ, login to BWZ

  • Check User Role Assignments: In the settings à Identity Provisioning
  • Within "Explore Role Assignments",enter the user ID experiencing the issue
  • Review the list of roles and permissions assigned to the user

Resolution: As described in scenario 1 navigation flow, maintain the joule roles in source system parameter run the job. This will synchronize the user and groups between ariba , IAS and BWZ. Make sure roles are assigned to users in ariba as well. Login to BWZ and verify the roles once again:

Common issues with Transactional flow and resolution:

Scenario 1:When user enters any transactional prompt in joule, user gets results as an informational data.

Sample prompts: List of recent purchases

Cause: This happens when ibn_target not found at the backend. In simple words, the formation synchronization did not happen between the include system despite the fact status of the formation is ready.  

Resolution: Login to global account, exclude and include Ariba buyer and sourcing system within the formations.

 

Scenario 2: When user enters a transactional prompt, there is no response

Cause: This happens when there is no dependencies added to the das-ias applications in CIS

Resolution:

  • Login to IAS
  • Navigate to applications and search for “das-ias” and look for the entry which is there for the sub-account which is created for joule
  • Navigate to dependency section, make sure to maintain APIs for Procurement and Sourcing applications

Delayed User synchronization between Ariba and Cloud identify service: IPS Jobs are getting stuck in running status for few hours  

Steps to run the Job:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision

Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba

 Click on job logs in IPS to check the status:

 Resolution:  By default the value configured for parameter (ariba.applications.group.members.page.size,ariba.applications.user.groups.page.size )  is 50, update it according to your need. If this doesn’t work, log a support ticket with SAP to increase the API rate limit.

Joule Icon Is Missing in Ariba despite completing all the configurations (running boosters, system added successfully in formations, SSO is enabled)

Resolution: Log a ticket with SAP to enable the feature PLUI 2928  at parent and child level

If you are facing any other issues, I would suggest to log a case with support team. To save time, make sure to share the logs and X-Correlation ID with support team. 

How to download the Joule Logs:

  • Login to Ariba application
  • Click on joule icon, reproduce the issue
  • Click on three bars --> settings  --> abouts --> download trances --> include message history --> download 

How to Get X Correlation ID?

  • Login to Ariba system
  • Enable logs:
    • Right-click on browser, select "Inspect"
    • once the Developer Tools are open, locate and click on the "Network" tab
  • Open Joule (keep the Developer Tools open)
  • Reproduce the issue you are experiencing
  • When the issue is reproduced:
    • in the Network tab, identify the most recent API call to Joule. This API call will be named with a UUID (for example: c1234b04-c313-42cd-9be3-a5efe9b0f2d1).
    • click on the API request and go to the "Headers" section;
    • scroll down to find and collect the following fields:
      • X-correlationid
      • Timestamp,
      • JouleDataCenter (you can find this in the "API Request URL")

Ariba-Joule support queues: you may choose the queue based on the nature of the error. 

✨Conclusion
The most common Ariba–Joule issues often stem from small but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone—and fixing these resolves the majority of navigation, transactional, and sync failures.
With the right configurations in place, Joule delivers the seamless, intelligent experience it’s designed for—helping customers unlock real value without post‑go‑live roadblocks. 🚀

entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.

entry.title: Two Ways to Build Agentic Reporting in SAP: Python Agent on SAP BTP vs. Joule Studio Agent
entry.content:
1. Introduction

In my previous blog post on Agentic Reporting, I talked about how modern reporting goes beyond dashboards — it requires systems that reason, explain, and act.

In this follow‑up, instead of going straight into a specific implementation, I want to answer a bigger question:

What are the different ways to build an AI Agent in the SAP ecosystem?

With SAP’s evolving AI landscape, you now have two powerful paths to build enterprise‑grade agents:

Option A — Build a Python Agent on SAP BTP

Custom, full‑code, flexible Agent service that integrates with SAP Datasphere via SQL or OData.

Option B — Build an Agent directly in SAP Joule Studio

A no‑code/low‑code agent builder inside SAP Build for reasoning, orchestration, and multi‑system actions.

Both options are valid. Both can support Agentic Reporting. But they differ significantly in architecture, flexibility, governance, and effort.

This post compares both, so you can choose the best path for your use case.

2. The Two Agent‑Building Paths Explained

Option A — Python Agent on SAP BTP (Full‑Code + Maximum Flexibility)

This is the classic approach where you build an application in Python, deploy it on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry or Kyma), and connect it to SAP Datasphere.

When is this ideal?

  • Heavy data processing
  • Custom ML/AI logic
  • Integration with complex non-SAP systems
  • You want maximum control over logic, dependencies, libraries
  • Advanced reasoning models + custom frameworks
  • You want to host an API that other tools can call (including Joule)

What your Python Agent can do

  • Connect to SAP Datasphere using:
    • SQL (hdbcli)
    • OData Analytical or Relational APIs
  • Execute complex logic (anomaly detection, simulations, scoring)
  • Write data back to Open SQL schema
  • Expose REST endpoints for other SAP systems and Joule agents to consume

Architecture Summary

  • Python app on BTP
  • Service bindings → HANA Cloud credentials via VCAP_SERVICES
  • OAuth → Datasphere OData access
  • HANA Cloud SQL → read/write via Open SQL schema
  • Python performs reasoning, actions, decisions

Option B — Joule Studio Agent (SAP-native no‑code Agent Builder)

SAP Joule Studio allows business teams and architects to build agents without needing to write any code.

These agents can:

  • Plan
  • Reason
  • Execute workflows
  • Call APIs (via Joule Skills)
  • Use SAP Knowledge Graph
  • Use Document Grounding
  • Orchestrate SAP and non‑SAP tools

When is this ideal?

  • You want an SAP-native, governed agent
  • You want quick setup (no DevOps, no runtime)
  • You want conversational + actionable agents
  • You want orchestration across SAP applications
  • You want to avoid managing Python runtimes
  • You want automatic reasoning and traceability

What your Joule Agent can do

  • Connect to SAP systems via destinations
  • Invoke Joule Skills (API calls, BTP actions)
  • Consume Datasphere data via OData APIs
  • Perform reasoning using SAP’s LLMs
  • Automate workflows in SuccessFactors, S/4HANA, Ariba, etc.

Architecture Summary

  • Joule Studio = Agent Builder
  • Uses Destinations for integration
  • Uses Skills & Actions for API calls
  • LLM-powered agentic reasoning

No deploy, no runtime maintenance

3. Comparing the Two Approaches

4. Which Option Should You Use?

When to Choose Python Agent on BTP

Technical Complexity Requirements:

  • Advanced algorithmic logic - When you need custom algorithms beyond standard business logic
  • Heavy transformations/ETL - For complex data processing workflows requiring full programmatic control 
  • External ML libraries - Integration with sklearn, PyTorch, transformers, or other specialized libraries 

Integration Scenarios:

  • API endpoint creation - When other systems need to call your agent as a service
  • Any system connectivity - Leveraging the "any API, any system" capability for non-SAP integrations 

When to Choose Joule Studio Agent

Rapid Development Needs:

  • Fast deployment with the "lower effort, no-code" approach 
  • Built-in agentic reasoning eliminating custom development overhead 

Enterprise Governance:

  • SAP-native governance & logging for compliance and auditability 
  • Built-in trace, executions, reasoning view for operational transparency 
  • Skill marketplace access for pre-built capabilities 

SAP Ecosystem Integration:

  • SAP-first integrations with S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba 
  • SAP Knowledge Graph + Documents for automatic grounding 
  • Workflow automation & orchestration as the primary use case

5. Conclusion:

SAP’s Agentic AI is evolving rapidly, and you now have two mature ways of building enterprise‑grade agents:

1. Python Agent on SAP BTP

Flexible, powerful, code-based, perfect for analytics and complex logic.

2️. Joule Studio Agent

SAP-native, governed, no-code, perfect for orchestration, reasoning, and functional automation.

Both are valid choices and can even work together.

 

Next in the series:

  • Blog #3: How to Build a Python Agent for Agentic Reporting (Datasphere + BTP)
  • Blog #4: How to Build an SAP-native Joule Agent for Agentic Reporting

Previous Blog in the series:

Blog #1: Agentic Reporting - The Next Evolution of SAP Reporting

entry.title: SAP 2026: Between standard power and individual competitive advantage
entry.content:
The world of SAP SE is changing. While SAP stock and the SAP dividend dominate the headlines of the financial press, companies are facing a much deeper question: How much standardization is necessary and how much individuality is permissible?

At Toraman Consulting, we follow a clear principle: The SAP standard is the foundation, but we don't condemn in-house developments . It's about maintaining a balance.

The foundation: Why the standard is more important today than ever

With the rise of SAP Cloud and innovations like Joule (SAP's new AI assistant), a clean core is becoming essential. Those who are too deeply entrenched in the system code of the old SAP R/3 world cannot fully leverage the advantages of S/4HANA and the new Joule agents . The standard ensures stability, update capability, and access to state-of-the-art functions in modules such as SAP MM , SAP SD , and SAP PP .

The optional part: Where in-house developments make the difference

But we also know that a company isn't successful simply by doing the exact same thing as everyone else. Real competitive advantages often lie in processes that are anything but run-of-the-mill.

Our philosophy at Toraman Consulting :

  • Weighing options instead of rejecting: We carefully examine where the standard is sufficient and where a targeted extension provides the decisive added value.
  • Securing advantages: If an in-house development strengthens your market position or makes your customers happier, then we will defend this solution.
  • Modern Development: We use the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to create individuality without contaminating the digital core.

Partnerships and Market Trends 2026

The market is responding to this need for expert knowledge. Large consulting firms like KPMG and Deloitte are heavily investing in strategic alliances to bridge the gap between standard software and customized AI solutions. As a partner in the SAP Open Ecosystem, we offer you precisely this flexibility: the security of a global market leader combined with the personalized support your business requires.

Conclusion: Balance wins

Whether you're building your SAP systems from scratch or need a complex migration strategy, let's discuss your options together. The goal is a system that's stable enough for the future, yet flexible enough to reflect your unique strengths.

entry.title: Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire 2026 Orlando
entry.content:
Unlocking Transformational Value at SAP Sapphire: Join the Exclusive ASUG Pre-Conference on the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio

SAP is entering a new era of services and support—one that is simpler, more intelligent, and designed to help organizations move faster toward cloud transformation and AI-driven innovation. To help customers understand and fully leverage this new approach, ASUG and SAP are hosting an exclusive full-day pre-conference at SAP Sapphire:

“Unlocking Transformational Value: A Practical Introduction to the New SAP Services & Support Portfolio.”

This immersive event brings together SAP leaders, product experts, and customers to walk through the modernized SAP Services & Support portfolio, explain the new tiered Success Plans, and demonstrate how organizations can accelerate cloud adoption, improve operations, and drive measurable business outcomes. With breakfast, lunch, expert sessions, and an exclusive showfloor tour, the day provides both strategic insight and practical guidance for anyone responsible for SAP transformation. Whether you are planning a cloud migration, optimizing hybrid landscapes, or exploring how AI can transform operations, this session will help you understand how to maximize value from your SAP investment.
Register Now!

What You’ll Learn

During this full-day experience, participants will explore:

  • The new SAP Success Plans: Foundational, Advanced, and Max

  • How AI-enabled services improve productivity and automation

  • Ways to optimize SAP operations and governance

  • How Development Services and co-innovation accelerate innovation

  • How Application Management and Sovereign Cloud support modern enterprise requirements

You’ll leave with practical guidance for building your SAP success strategy for 2026 and beyond. Register Now!

Full Agenda May 11 | Orlando

8:30–9:00 AM — Welcome

Welcome & Setting the Stage

Speakers:
Geoff Scott, CEO and Chief Community Champion, ASUG
Thomas Saueressig, Member of the Executive Board of SAP SE, Head of Customer Services & Delivery

Kick off the day with ASUG and SAP leadership as they outline why SAP simplified its Services & Support portfolio. Learn what customers asked for and how the new model supports cloud adoption, AI-driven innovation, and predictable, business-aligned outcomes.

9:00–9:45 AM — Keynote Session

The New SAP Services & Support Portfolio: Simpler, Smarter, Predictable

Speakers:
Thomas Pfiester, Head of Customer Engagement & Adoption and Member of the Extended Board of SAP SE
Stefan Steinle, EVP and Head Global Customer Support, SAP
Uwe Grigoleit, SVP, Head of SAP Customer Evolution and Portfolio

Discover the structure and strategy behind SAP’s new Services & Support portfolio. This keynote introduces SAP Success Plans (Foundational, Advanced, and Max) alongside Development Services, Application Management, Professional Services, and SAP Sovereign Cloud. Learn how AI-enabled services simplify operations, increase predictability, and accelerate adoption, innovation, and business value.

9:45–9:55 AM — Break

9:55–10:25 AM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Foundational for Smart Adoption and Enablement

Speakers:
Rohan Patel, Global Head of Support, SAP Procurement
Jens Bernotat, Global Vice President Strategy, Portfolio, Global Customer Support, SAP

Learn what’s included in the SAP Foundational Success Plan, including SAP Enterprise Support, digital guidance, SAP Cloud ALM, and best practices. This session highlights how organizations can onboard faster, maintain system stability, and continuously improve operations across their SAP cloud landscape.

10:25–11:00 AM — Spotlight Session

Transforming the Cloud Journey with SAP Cloud ALM and Next-Generation Agents

Speakers:
Björn Braemer, SVP & Program Executive, Customer Evolution Program, SAP
Thorsten Spihlmann, Head of Business Development for Data Transformation, SAP

SAP Cloud ALM is evolving into a Transformation Execution Platform, helping organizations accelerate cloud transformation. Discover how an enhanced AI toolchain and agentic AI capabilities support configuration, migration, data preparation, and automated testing—reducing manual effort and enabling faster, smarter transformation journeys.

11:00–11:45 AM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Advanced for Guided, Predictable Transformation

Speakers:
Peter Roberts, SVP & Chief Product Officer, Advanced Success Plan, SAP

The Advanced Success Plan provides expert-guided engagement for complex transformation programs. Learn how tailored guidance, architectural reviews, readiness checks, and proactive insights help ensure predictable adoption and business-aligned outcomes across regions and teams.

11:45 AM–12:45 PM — Lunch Break

12:45–1:30 PM — Success Plan Deep Dive

Max for Strategic Partnership and Business-Critical Success

Speakers:
Sebastien Denes, Global Vice President, Chief Product Officer Max Success Plan, SAP

Explore SAP’s highest level of engagement with the Max Success Plan. Learn how dedicated experts, proactive governance, AI-driven insights, and mission-critical operations support help enterprises protect business continuity, accelerate innovation, and drive strategic outcomes at scale.

1:30–2:15 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Co-Innovating with SAP Development Services: From AI Ideas to Impact

Speakers:
Sindhu Gangadharan, Head, Customer Innovation Services; MD, SAP Labs India

Discover how SAP Development Services partners with customers to build tailored solutions—from generative AI use cases to agentic AI scenarios. This session includes a real-world example demonstrating how custom AI innovation improved efficiency, decision-making, and measurable business outcomes.

2:15–3:00 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Next Generation Application Management for Predictive, Intelligent, and Automated Operations

Speakers:
Lalit Patil, EVP & Chief Technology Officer, SAP
Vinay Hiriganahalli, SVP, Head of Cloud Application Services, SAP

Learn how SAP Application Management is transforming operations from reactive support to predictive, AI-driven optimization. Through artificial intelligence, machine learning, and cognitive automation, organizations can move beyond issue resolution toward proactive performance management and intelligent operations.

3:00–3:15 PM — Break

3:15–3:45 PM — Portfolio Extension Deep Dive

Secure, Compliant Innovation for Regulated Industries and Public Sector

Speaker:
Peter Keller, VP, Sovereign Cloud Architecture, SAP

Explore how SAP Sovereign Cloud helps organizations meet strict data residency, compliance, and security requirements while still accelerating innovation. The session covers architecture, use cases, and practical steps for adopting sovereign cloud models across regulated industries and the public sector.

3:45–4:30 PM — Strategy Session

Build Your SAP Success Strategy: Practical Guidance and Roadmap 2026

Speakers:
Uwe Grigoleit, SVP, Head of SAP Customer Evolution and Portfolio, SAP
Patrick Wells, Global Head of SAP Services Portfolio Evolution, SAP

This practical session helps you translate insights from the day into a clear SAP engagement strategy.

Learn how to:

  • Match business priorities with the right Success Plan

  • Identify ROI drivers

  • Apply best practices for adoption

Build a practical roadmap for 2026

Participants will leave with a template to assess their current setup and define their future SAP success strategy.
Register Now!

Exclusive Bonus: Guided SAP Sapphire Showfloor Tour & F1 Experience

4:30–6:00 PM

Discover Sapphire Like an Insider

Wrap up the day with a curated, expert-led tour of the SAP Sapphire showfloor. Navigating Sapphire can be overwhelming—this guided experience ensures you don’t miss the innovations and demos most relevant to your SAP journey.

Tour highlights include:

  • SAP Experience Center

  • Artificial Intelligence innovations

  • Data & Analytics

  • Business Applications

  • Technology Platform

  • Business Transformation

  • Industry Center

  • Services & Support Center

And to finish the day: Experience the SAP Formula 1 racing simulator and connect with SAP experts in an interactive setting. Register Now!

Why You Should Attend

This ASUG pre-conference offers a unique opportunity to learn directly from SAP leaders and customers about the future of services and support.

Participants will gain:

✔ A clear understanding of SAP’s new Services & Support portfolio
✔ Practical insights into AI-driven operations and transformation
✔ Real customer experiences and best practices
✔ Direct access to SAP experts
✔ A structured strategy for maximizing value from SAP investments

Seats are limited — secure your spot and start SAP Sapphire with insights that will shape your transformation journey. Register Now!

entry.title: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content:
Last week we had completed the system set up and also created a package with your community ID. We had also gone through the help document on SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities.

Now let us dive into the world of ABAP AI by creating an application where Joule will guide you during each step.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Task 1: Create a ABAP RAP application using 'OData UI Service from Scratch’ wizard

It can be any application of your choice preferably non enterprise related to keep the challenge simple and more interesting. One field should be an amount field and another one a date field so that we can leverage J4D(Joule for Developers) to add some logic in the subsequent weeks. You should have 3 more fields which will be used to try out some of the J4D features.

Note: It is recommended to create all the fields while creating the app itself to avoid any manual coding later.

Example:- You can create a book store application consisting of fields ID, Title, Author, Price, Published Date, Stock, Discounted Price, BookAge. Here the last 3 fields can be used to try out the Joule for Developers (J4D) features. You are free to choose any dataset and logic combinations.

  1. Right click on the package ZAC_<your community ID> created and select ‘Generate ABAP Repository Objects’Generate ABAP Repository Objects’
  2. In the below popup that appears choose ‘Odata UI Service from Scratch’ and click on ‘Next’ two times.Odata UI Service from Scratch
  3. Click ‘Next’ again.Click Next
  4. In the next screen make sure you get the Joule Help to the right of Wizard popup. Here in the section ‘Ask Joule to update the fields’ can provide your prompts to create the application you like by specifying the fields and other details. Hit enter. Note: It is better to create all objects ending with your <community ID> to avoid any naming conflicts. Try to specify this in the prompt.Joule option in WizardNote: Make sure to select the option -  ‘Transactional with Draft’ highlighted in the above screenshot is selected to enable ‘Create’ option in the application getting created. For single entity sometimes it is set as ‘Without Draft’.
  5. Joule will now propose the Entity and Fields based on your prompt. Please note that we still should verify whether the suggestions are accurate for our requirements. Here you can notice that sometimes Joule suggests to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional Without Draft’, we can handle this in next step.Joule Field Proposal
  6. Once you are satisfied click on ‘Accept’ and you can see that the ‘Artifact Suffix’ if specified and the ‘Project Name’ gets added as shown in the below screenshot.           Here remember to change the Service Configuration to ‘Transactional with Draft’ and click ‘Next’.Joule Accept Proposal
  7. Joule lists the artifacts that will be created. Verify them again and click on ‘Next’ and then ‘Finish’ on the next screen. 
  8. Refresh the package and check all the artifacts have been created. Publish the Service Binding and preview the application.
  9. The application will open and should have a ‘Create’ button. Here you can either create a data generator class and add some simple logic to fill data / Use the ‘Create’ option in the app to create some sample records. ( Please create 4-5 entries in any case ). You can also adjust the metadata if you like.Application Created

For more information: https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/use-odata-ui-service-from-scratch-wizard?locale=en-US

Task 2: Add a simple logic in the CDS data definition

  1. Add some simple logic in the CDS data definition for example to provide 2% discount on actual Price and fill another field say ‘DiscountedPrice’ with the discounted value for all records in the database.

    Note: The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

  2. Now that one of the fields was enhanced, you may need to recreate the draft table ending with _D### before launching the application again. If you do not face any issue you can skip this step.

    To do that, go to the Project Explorer and open the behavior definition click on the icon as shown on the picture to follow the quick fix. Save and activate the draft table.

    Recreate Draft Table

Task 3: Use Joule->Explain capability to understand code

Use ‘Explain’ capability of Joule to get detailed explanation of the code added.

Hint: Please add few lines of code like a simple CASE statement in CDS data definition to test Explain feature.

You may also watch this video to know more about how to use Joule->Explain feature.

We will be back next week with another feature of Joule AI Capability. Stay Tuned!!

entry.title: My SAP Business AI Certification Journey
entry.content:
When I started these SAP Business AI courses (Discovering SAP Business AI, Positioning SAP Business AI and Boosting AI-driven Business Transformation with Joule Agents), I expected a standard product overview. What I got instead was a completely new way of thinking about enterprise technology, AI strategy, and how to have meaningful conversations with clients about digital transformation.

This blog is my attempt to share what I learned, how I passed my certification, and what surprised me along the way — for anyone about to take the same journey.

SAP Certification Name: SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business AI Solutions as part of SAP Business Suite (C_BCBAI)

1. What the Certification Covers

The certification spans three SAP Learning courses, each building on the previous one. Together they cover the full SAP Business AI story — from strategy to technical architecture to real-world deployment.

Course

Focus

Key Learning

Discovering SAP Business AI

Why AI? What is SAP's strategy?

The SAP Flywheel — how Applications, Data and AI reinforce each other

Positioning SAP Business AI

How to position AI to different personas

Joule, AI Foundation, Responsible AI, Sustainability — and how each lands with CIO, CFO, COO

Joule Agents & Transformation

How AI agents actually work in business

Agentic AI, Business Data Cloud, Knowledge Graph, AI Agent Hub — the full technical picture

2. The Three Things That Changed How I Think

A. AI Without Data Is Just Noise

The biggest insight from Course 1 was simple but profound: AI is only as good as the data feeding it. Before these courses, I used to talk about AI features. Now I start every conversation with the data foundation.

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is what makes AI reliable — connecting all systems using zero-copy federation, preserving business context through the Semantic Layer, and keeping data sovereign in each region. Without BDC, AI outputs are guesswork.

B. Joule Is Not Just a Chatbot

Before the certification, I described Joule as "SAP's AI assistant." After it, I describe it very differently. Joule is an omnipresent AI Copilot with 1,800+ skills, covering 80% of SAP's most-used transactions, available in 11 languages, and capable of proactively acting before users even ask.

And then there are Joule Agents — a completely different level. Agents reason, plan, and execute multi-step workflows autonomously. The Cash Management Agent saves finance teams up to 70% of manual reconciliation time. The Production Planning Agent autonomously checks material and capacity. 

C. Responsible AI Is a Commercial Accelerator

I used to treat ethics and compliance as a defensive topic — something to address when clients raised concerns. The certification changed that. SAP's Responsible AI framework is actually a competitive advantage.

  • Client data never leaves SAP's governed architecture — never used to train third-party models
  • Every Joule action is fully auditable — no black-box decisions
  • Data sovereignty built in — GDPR in Europe, PDPA in Asia, automatic
  • ISO 42001 certified for AI governance — internationally recognised standard
  • Human-in-the-loop by design — AI recommends, humans approve critical decisions.

3. How I Applied the Learning in the Certification

The certification assessment is scenario-based — which I found genuinely challenging and genuinely useful. Here is what helped me prepare and what I would tell anyone taking it next.

Scenario Type

What It Tests

My Approach

Client objection handling

Can you position BDC and Joule against a "we don't need to change" mindset?

Memorised the control tower analogy — ERPs are engines, BDC is the control tower. Never replace, always layer.

Persona-based positioning

Which message lands with which persona — CIO vs CFO vs COO?

Built a mental matrix: CIO = integration + security, CFO = ROI + reconciliation time, COO = supply chain speed

Technical architecture

How does zero-copy federation work? What is the Knowledge Graph?

Used real examples: Knowledge Graph maps PO to Supplier to Invoice — grounding AI in business context, preventing hallucinations

Responsible AI scenarios

What happens to client data? How is compliance maintained?

Led with ISO 42001, data sovereignty, human-in-the-loop — not features, but governance principles

The exam rewards consultative thinking, not product recall. The question is rarely "what does Joule do?" It is almost always "given this client situation, what would you recommend and why?" Thinking like a consultant — not a salesperson — is the key.

4. What I Gained Beyond the Certificate

Certifications give you a credential. Good certifications give you a new lens. This one gave me both.

  • Confidence in client conversations — I can now discuss AI architecture, data sovereignty, and agent capabilities without hesitation or vagueness.
  • A structured positioning framework — I know which message to lead with for each persona and which technical detail to deploy for each objection.
  • Real examples that land — not "SAP has AI agents" but "the Cash Management Agent saves your finance team 70% of manual reconciliation time — here is exactly how."
  • A sustainability angle I was not expecting — the Green Ledger and Sustainability Tower capabilities genuinely surprised me with their depth and maturity.
  • A new appreciation for data — the courses reinforced that AI is a data story first and a technology story second. That reframing has changed how I approach every client engagement.
  • A structured positioning framework — I know which message to lead with for each persona and which technical detail to deploy for each objection.
  • Real examples that land — not "SAP has AI agents" but "the Cash Management Agent saves your finance team 70% of manual reconciliation time — here is exactly how."
  • A sustainability angle I was not expecting — the Green Ledger and Sustainability Tower capabilities genuinely surprised me with their depth and maturity.
  • A new appreciation for data — the courses reinforced that AI is a data story first and a technology story second. That reframing has changed how I approach every client engagement.

The most valuable moment in the entire certification was a scenario-based roleplay where I had to respond to a sceptical client who said "our ERP works fine, why change it?" Every concept from all three courses came together in that one moment. That is when I knew the learning had actually landed.

Final Thought

If you are considering this certification — do it. Not just for the badge, but for the way it reshapes how you think about AI, data, and enterprise transformation.

And when you sit down with your next client who says "we are curious about AI but not sure where to start" — you will know exactly what to say.

Start with the data. Layer the intelligence. Let the Flywheel turn.

If this was useful, please like and share. Questions or your own experiences? Drop them in the comments below. Happy to connect with fellow SAP learners.

entry.title: From setup to value: Activating SAP Joule for Developers
entry.content:
AI is changing how developers build software.  Not in theory, in their daily workflow.

That’s exactly where SAP Joule comes in.

In our upcoming session, “From setup to value: Activating SAP Joule for Developers,” we’ll explore how generative AI is becoming part of the SAP development experience.  Think of it as moving from “AI sounds interesting” to “AI is actually helping me code and build faster.”

The thing is, activation is often the step that slows teams down.  What needs to be configured?  What are the prerequisites?  Where do you even begin?

We’ll walk through all of that.  Step by step.

Plus, you’ll see how Joule connects into the broader SAP Business Technology Platform environment and what partners should know when enabling it for development teams and customers.

Honestly, if you're building, integrating, or innovating on SAP solutions, this is one of those sessions that can save you a lot of guesswork.

Join us live and learn how to bring AI directly into the developer experience.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013283.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPAI #DeveloperExperience #EnterpriseAI #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Unveiling a Fresh Look of SAP Learning’s Artificial Intelligence Page + Beyond Hype Live Sessions
entry.content:
Have you checked out the revamped Artificial Intelligence page on SAP Learning? If not, now is the perfect time to explore its sleek new design and discover a wealth of resources to empower your AI journey. Whether you're curious about the basics of Artificial Intelligence or eager to dive into SAP Business AI solutions, this updated page is your gateway to actionable knowledge.

But that’s not all—SAP Learning is taking education beyond the hype with "Beyond Hype: The AI Series", a series of live, fast-paced 45-minute sessions designed to help you not just talk about AI but use it with confidence. These sessions, led by inspiring Joule and AI Foundation experts, offer tangible, applied learning to help you adopt and apply SAP Business AI solutions effectively.

Why Join Beyond Hype?

  • Practical Learning: These aren’t just theoretical talks; these sessions are designed to help you turn AI concepts into real-world applications.
  • Expert Guidance: Gain insights from top AI minds who understand what it takes to leverage AI within SAP solutions.
  • Convenient Format: Short, engaging sessions that fit into your busy schedule.
  • On-Demand Access: Missed a session? Don’t worry—you can catch up with recordings anytime.

The revamped page highlights these sessions with an eye-catching banner that links directly to the Beyond Hype Session Catalogue, your one-stop destination for registering for upcoming live sessions and accessing previously recorded ones. It’s all about seamless navigation and empowering learning.

Head over to Artificial Intelligence at SAP Learning to experience the new look and feel and take your first step toward mastering AI. Start using it today with Beyond Hype!

entry.title: End to End guide to setup Joule, SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Work Zone on SAP BTP
entry.content:
1. Introduction

Modern enterprises rely on multiple SAP and third-party applications to run their business processes. However, navigating across different systems and extracting insights quickly can be challenging for users. To address this, SAP is embedding artificial intelligence directly into its enterprise solutions through Joule, SAP’s AI copilot.

Joule transforms how users interact with SAP systems by enabling natural language interactions, automating routine tasks, and surfacing relevant business insights instantly. Combined with SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) and SAP Build Work Zone, organizations can build intelligent workflows, integrate APIs, and deliver AI-powered business experiences through a unified digital workspace.

1.1 What You Will Learn in This Blog

In this blog, we will walk through the end-to-end setup and integration of Joule with SAP Build services on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). You will learn:

  • An overview of Joule and its role as SAP’s AI copilot

  • The architecture and environment required to run Joule on SAP BTP

  • How to configure SAP Build Process Automation and create Joule skills

  • The setup steps required in SAP BTP, including Cloud Identity Services and service entitlements

  • How Joule skills interact with APIs, workflows, and destinations

  • How SAP Build Work Zone enables users to access Joule-powered experiences through a unified workspace

1.2 Joule: SAP’s AI Copilot

Joule is SAP’s AI copilot designed to simplify how users interact with enterprise applications. It provides a conversational interface that helps employees complete tasks, retrieve insights, and navigate across SAP systems more efficiently.

Designed as a companion for the Intelligent Enterprise, Joule offers role-based assistance across the SAP solution portfolio. Through natural language interactions, users can search information, access relevant applications, and perform business transactions without navigating complex menus.

By integrating directly with business applications, Joule enhances productivity, improves decision-making, and streamlines daily operations.

1.3 Environment and Architecture

Joule is built using modern cloud-native architecture on SAP BTP.

Key architectural characteristics include:

  • Runs on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry
  • Built as a multi-tenant application, ensuring strict data isolation between customers
  • Secure and enterprise-ready architecture
  • Compliance with AI ethics guidelines and GDPR standards

This architecture enables organizations to safely leverage generative AI capabilities within their SAP landscapes.

1.4 Key Features

  • Deep SAP Integration
    Joule is embedded directly within SAP applications, allowing users to interact with business processes without leaving their working environment.
  • Conversational User Interface
    The assistant provides a conversational UI built with SAP Fiori-compliant controls, enabling intuitive interactions.
  • Tailored Capabilities
    Joule adapts its assistant capabilities based on the SAP solutions implemented within the organization.
  • Continuous Innovation
    New capabilities and improvements are delivered automatically as part of SAP’s cloud updates.

2. Introduction to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

SAP Build Process Automation is a low-code platform on SAP BTP that enables organizations to automate business processes, integrate systems, and build intelligent workflows.

SBPA combines multiple capabilities in one solution:

  • Workflow automation
  • Business rules management
  • AI-assisted process development
  • Joule skill creation using Joule studio

Within the Joule ecosystem, SAP Build Process Automation plays a critical role by enabling developers to create skills and workflows that Joule can trigger through natural language interactions.

For example, a Joule request such as: “Create a purchase request for office supplies” can trigger an automated workflow built in SBPA that interacts with backend systems.

2.1 Introduction to Joule Studio

Joule Studio is the development environment used to create and configure Joule skills and actions.

It allows developers to:

  • Integrate APIs
  • Define conversational capabilities
  • Build workflows
  • Connect enterprise systems
  • Test AI-assisted interactions

Joule Studio works closely with SAP Build Process Automation, enabling developers to design intelligent workflows that Joule can execute when users submit requests.

2.2 Initial Setup on SAP BTP

Before configuring Joule and related services, several prerequisites must be met in the SAP BTP cockpit.

Prerequisites

Ensure the following service plans are available in your global account:

A subaccount must be created in a Joule-supported data center region.

Step 1: Configure Custom Identity Provider (Cloud Identity Services)

Check if a Cloud Identity tenant already exists. If available, note the tenant URL: https://<tenant>.accounts400.ondemand.com. If not, create a new tenant.

Assign Entitlement

Navigate to: Subaccount → Entitlements → Add Service Plan→ Cloud Identity Services→ additional-tenant plan

Create Instance

Service Marketplace → Cloud Identity Services → Create Instance

Activate Tenant 

Activate the tenant through the email sent by SAP.

Step 2: Create a User in the Subaccount

Navigate to: Security → Users → Create User

Provide:

  • Name
  • Email
  • Identity Provider (Cloud Identity tenant)

Assign the required role collections to the user.

Step 3: Configure SAP Build Process Automation

Establish Trust

Subaccount → Security → Trust Configuration → Establish Trust

Enter the Cloud Identity tenant URL.

Add Entitlement

Add the service: SAP Build Process Automation – build-default plan

Create Instance

Service Marketplace → Process Automation → Create Instance

Assign Roles

Assign the following role collections to the Cloud Identity user:

  • Process Automation Admin
  • Process Automation Developer
  • Process Automation Participant
  • Process Automation Viewer

Step 4: Configure SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition)

Add Entitlement

Add the service: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – standard plan

Create Instance

Service Marketplace → SAP Build Work Zone → Create Instance

Assign Roles

Assign role collections such as:

  • Launchpad_Admin
  • Launchpad_User

Step 5: Run the Joule Booster

Navigate to: Global Account → Boosters

Search for: Setting Up Joule and start the booster.

Select Configuration

Choose:

  • das-application – standard plan
  • Target subaccount

Select products and integrations such as:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone

Resolving Prerequisite Errors

If prerequisites are missing, ensure the Cloud Identity tenant appears in:

BTP Cockpit → Systems

If not visible:

  • Request access to the Cloud Reporting tool: https://reporting.ondemand.com/sap/crp/cdo
  • Search for your custom tenant and copy the CIS Tenant ID. It may take a day to reflect the tenant idon cloud reporting tool after the custom tenant creation
  • Add it under: Global Account → Systems → Service Owner View→ Add Cloud Identity services system→ tenant ID

Assign Joule Roles

Create a role collection: Joule Administrator

Add the following roles from the das-application service:

  • end_user
  • extensibility_developer
  • tenant_admin

Assign the role collection to your Cloud Identity user.

Final Outcome

After completing these steps:

  • Joule is provisioned in your BTP subaccount
  • Identity trust is established
  • SBPA and Build Work Zone are integrated
  • Required roles are assigned

2.3 Joule Skill Development using SAP Build Process Automation:

SAP BTP Destinations

  • This is where you register external APIs that your Joule skill will call.
  • A destination in SAP BTP acts as a connection configuration (e.g., endpoint URL, authentication type, credentials) for external systems or APIs.
  • Example: A REST API that retrieves employee details or triggers a business workflow.

Action: You first create or register the API endpoint as a destination in SAP BTP.

Environment (in SAP Build Process Automation)

  • The environment is the runtime where Joule skills are deployed and executed.
  • It is linked to the BTP destinations you registered earlier.

Action:

  • You attach the environment to your destinations.
  • This ensures your Joule skill has permission to call those APIs at runtime.
  • The environment can also be shared with SAP Build Work Zone or Joule Standalone.

Joule Action

  • This is where you define the REST API calls your Joule skill will use.
  • You configure the GET/POST/PUT/DELETE methods, define request/response payloads, and test the API output.

Action:

  • Create a Joule Action that calls the registered destination API.
  • Validate that the API response works as expected (just like postman)

Project (Joule Skill Workflow)

  • Within SAP Build Process Automation, you create a Project that designs the skill logic (workflow) for your Joule skill.
  • You integrate your Joule Action (from the previous step) inside this workflow.
  • This project defines how the skill behaves when invoked — the steps, logic, and output.

Action:

  • Design the workflow for your skill.
  • Integrate your Joule Action using REST APIs to handle inputs/outputs.

SAP Build Work Zone
SAP Build Work Zone provides a unified digital workspace where users access business applications. It enables organizations to create business sites that bring together:

  • SAP applications (such as SAP S/4HANA)
  • Custom-built applications
  • Third-party solutions

These applications can run in the cloud or on-premises, but are delivered through a single unified user experience. Once Joule skills are created and deployed, they can be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone so users can invoke them directly from the interface.

  • This is where your application is hosted.
  • It provides the front-end interface through which users interact with Joule-integrated applications.

Action:

  • Once your Joule skill is ready, it can be integrated and accessed from within the SAP Build Work Zone.
  • This integration allows users to invoke the skill directly from the UI.

Joule Standalone

Joule can also operate independently outside of SAP Build Work Zone. In standalone mode, it still uses the same SAP Build Process Automation environment and BTP destinations, allowing Joule skills and integrations to function across different interfaces.

Shared Environment

Joule Standalone and SAP Build Work Zone share the same SAP Build Process Automation environment, which contains the configured destinations and deployed workflows. This shared setup allows developers to build and manage integrations once and reuse them across multiple interfaces, ensuring consistency and easier maintenance.

Workflow Summary:

  1. Register API: Create a destination in SAP BTP for the external API.
  2. Attach Environment: Link the environment with BTP destinations.
  3. Configure Joule Actions: Define API calls (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE) and validate responses.
  4. Build Project: Create workflow (skill logic) integrating the Joule Action.
  5. Deploy Environment: Joule deploys the skill here using attached destinations.
  6. Integrate UI: Expose the skill through SAP Build Work Zone or Joule standalone.

3. Introduction to SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone is a modern digital experience platform that enables organizations to create unified, role-based, and personalized access to applications, business processes, and information across both SAP and non-SAP systems.

It delivers a launchpad-style user experience, allowing users to access SAP Fiori applications, custom-built applications (such as CAP apps), third-party web applications, documents, and collaboration tools through a single unified interface.

By centralizing application access and content delivery, SAP Build Work Zone simplifies the user experience and improves productivity across the enterprise.

SAP Build Work Zone Editions

SAP Build Work Zone is available in two editions designed for different business needs.

Edition

Purpose

Standard Edition

A lightweight, launchpad-focused solution ideal for internal business applications and SAP Fiori experiences.

Advanced Edition

A comprehensive digital workplace solution built on SAP BTP and Cloud Identity Services, enabling intranet capabilities, collaboration features, and enterprise-wide content management.

Most application-centric implementations — including Joule integrations — typically start with the Standard Edition.

3.1 Core Components of SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone is composed of several key components that help organize applications, manage access, and design user experiences.

Sites

A Site represents the main digital workspace or portal where users access applications and content. It defines the overall structure and experience of the workspace, including navigation, pages, branding, and available applications.

Key characteristics:

  • Acts as the entry point for users
  • Contains pages, apps, and navigation menus
  • Supports branding and layout customization
  • Can be internal-only (Standard Edition) or public-facing (Advanced Edition)

Organizations often create different sites for departments, business units, or use cases.

Site Directory

The Site Directory is the central place where administrators manage and access all created sites.

From the Site Directory, administrators can:

  • Create new sites
  • Manage existing sites
  • Assign site administrators
  • Configure navigation and structure
  • Launch the site editor

It acts as the starting point for designing and managing digital workspaces within SAP Build Work Zone.

Content Manager

The Content Manager is the central administration tool used to manage and organize all site content within SAP Build Work Zone. It allows administrators to configure applications, pages, navigation structures, and access permissions for users.

Through the Content Manager, administrators manage several key components that define how content is presented and accessed in the workspace.

Apps

Apps represent the applications that users interact with inside SAP Build Work Zone. These applications can originate from various sources and are integrated into the workspace to provide a unified access experience.

Apps can include:

  • SAP Fiori applications
  • Custom-built applications such as CAP applications
  • External web applications
  • Embedded third-party solutions

Applications are typically integrated using:

  • SAP BTP Destinations
  • HTML5 Application Repository

This integration approach enables users to access applications seamlessly, regardless of whether they run on SAP BTP, external platforms, or on-premise systems.

Pages

Pages define the layout and structure in which applications and content are displayed within a site. Each page can contain multiple sections that organize different types of content and applications.

Common page elements include:

  • Tiles – clickable application launch icons
  • Cards – dynamic widgets that display real-time data or insights
  • Sections – logical groupings used to organize tiles and cards on a page

Pages can be created in two ways:

  • Custom-designed pages created by administrators to meet specific business needs
  • System-generated pages, such as default home pages or task-related pages like My Inbox

This flexible page structure allows organizations to design role-based and personalized digital experiences for their users.

Spaces

Spaces provide a high-level navigation structure within a site. They group related pages together and help organize the workspace for different business roles or functions.

For example, organizations might create spaces for:

  • Finance
  • Human Resources
  • Procurement
  • Analytics

Each space can contain multiple pages that organize relevant applications and content.

Catalogs

Catalogs are collections of applications that can be assigned to roles. They act as containers that group related apps together, making it easier to manage and distribute content across users.

Administrators typically organize catalogs based on business functions, departments, or solution areas.

Roles

Roles control access to catalogs and the applications within them. When roles are assigned to users, they automatically gain access to the catalogs and apps associated with those roles.

When SAP Build Work Zone is first subscribed to a subaccount, a default role called Subaccount_Everyone is automatically created. This role includes all users within the subaccount by default. Administrators can use this role to assign common content that should be accessible to every user, such as:

  • Home pages
  • General applications
  • Company-wide tools
  • Shared informational content

Additional custom roles can be created to support department-specific or role-based access control.

Channel Manager

The Channel Manager is used to import and manage business content from external providers such as SAP systems.

It allows administrators to subscribe to content channels, which automatically bring preconfigured applications into Work Zone.

Common use cases include importing:

  • SAP S/4HANA Fiori apps
  • SAP SuccessFactors apps
  • SAP Business Technology Platform content

Once a channel is subscribed, the apps become available in the Content Manager, where they can be assigned to catalogs and roles. This significantly simplifies the process of integrating SAP application content into Work Zone.

Settings and Joule Enablement

The Settings section in SAP Build Work Zone allows administrators to configure system-level features and integrations. From the settings panel, administrators can configure:

  • Site branding and theming
  • Authentication and identity integration
  • Feature toggles and extensions
  • Joule AI integration

Enabling Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

To enable Joule within Work Zone:

  1. Navigate to Site Manager
  2. Open Settings
  3. Enable the Joule AI Copilot option
     
  4. Ensure the Joule service (das-application) is correctly configured in SAP BTP
  5. Assign required Joule roles to users
  6. Once enabled, users can interact with Joule directly within the Work Zone interface, allowing them to:
  • Search for information
  • Trigger workflows
  • Access applications through natural language
  • Retrieve insights from SAP systems

This integration brings AI-powered assistance directly into the digital workspace, further improving productivity and user experience.

entry.title: Ready to build AI that changes everything? Are you prepared to design next‑level AI?
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence is evolving fast—but how we design AI matters just as much as the technology itself. If you’re passionate about building impactful, responsible, and user‑centric AI solutions within the SAP ecosystem, there’s a standout course now available on SAP Learning: 👉 Designing Agentic Systems with a Human-Centered Approach

This course teaches you how to co‑design AI with humans in mind, applying proven design principles, responsible AI practices, and practical frameworks to create solutions that are intuitive, safe, ethical, and valuable.

Whether you work in product management, UX, consulting, development, or innovation, you’ll learn how to:

  • Map real business scenarios where agentic AI adds value
  • Integrate human-centered design into every stage of AI solutioning
  • Understand how agents think, decide, and act in SAP contexts
  • Apply responsible AI guidelines to ensure safe and ethical outcomes
  • Prototype AI-driven agents aligned with user needs and workflows

This learning experience is perfect for:

  • SAP consultants and architects
  • Solution and product designers
  • Innovation, AI, and UX practitioners
  • Developers exploring agent-based AI
  • Anyone driving digital transformation initiatives

No heavy technical background required—just curiosity and a passion for designing meaningful AI experiences.
This course equips you with the mindset and methods to design agentic systems responsibly, creatively, and effectively—skills increasingly sought after across industries. 
Take the next step in your AI learning journey—become a designer of AI that truly works for people.
🔗 Start Learning Now 👉 Enroll here:

entry.title: How SAP Joule Will Transform User Support in the Fieldglass Landscape
entry.content:
Anyone who has spent years working with SAP Fieldglass—whether on the customer side running a large MSP program or, like me today, as a Principal Functional Delivery Consultant at SAP—knows one recurring challenge all too well: users who only interact with the system occasionally.

Hiring managers, project leads, timesheet approvers, budget owners—roles like these often touch Fieldglass only sporadically. Yet this exact user group generates a disproportionately high volume of support needs. And the reasons are easy to understand:

 

  1. Low familiarity with the system,
  2. Changing requirements,
  3. Process complexity,
  4. and despite well‑written user guides, a consistently high demand for hands‑on help.

 

Why these users—and the PMOs supporting them—will soon see massive relief

With SAP Joule, the generative AI assistant embedded deeply within the SAP ecosystem, the Fieldglass domain is about to experience a significant shift. Many of the inquiries that today require manual intervention by PMOs, administrators, or program support teams will soon be handled directly and intelligently by Joule.

And this, in my view, is the real breakthrough:

Anything that is done rarely benefits tremendously from conversational, guided assistance.

As AI adoption accelerates across enterprises, the willingness of users to rely on an AI assistant is growing rapidly—making Joule’s impact in Fieldglass almost inevitable.

 

Everyday Use Cases Joule Will Effortlessly Take Over

The list of tasks that Joule can simplify or automate in Fieldglass is extensive. Here are some of the most common scenarios from real project experience:

 

  1. “Where can I find my timesheet?”

A timeless classic.

Despite simple navigation, many users inevitably get lost—or look in the wrong place.

Joule will:

 

  1. provide the exact link to the timesheet,
  2. explain its status (“submitted”, “rejected”, “awaiting approval”),
  3. and suggest the next action when necessary.

 

  1. Template questions (Job Postings, SOWs, Work Orders)

Tasks done only a few times a year always produce questions.

Joule will:

 

  1. propose the right template based on role and context,
  2. explain its use case,
  3. and clarify differences between template variations.

 

  1. Process guidance: “How do I adjust a worker’s rate?”, “How do I extend a Work Order?”

These are frequent questions, but often detailed and nuanced.

Joule will:

 

  1. walk users through every step,
  2. highlight relevant configurations,
  3. and prevent typical mistakes.

 

  1. Interpreting and resolving error messages

Many support tickets stem from misunderstanding simple system warnings or validations.

Joule will:

 

  1. analyze the error in real time,
  2. explain the cause in plain language,
  3. and offer immediate solutions.

 

  1. Navigation support

Especially in global organizations with complex roles and views, users can quickly feel overwhelmed.

Joule will:

 

  1. provide contextual guidance,
  2. explain menus and actions,
  3. and reduce the learning curve dramatically.

 

 

Why Joule Is More Than a Convenience Feature

From years of Fieldglass implementations and customer advisory roles, I see three major benefits:

  1. Significant workload reduction for PMO and admin teams

A large portion of repetitive, manual support work will diminish—allowing teams to focus on strategic improvements rather than daily troubleshooting.

  1. Substantial improvements in user experience

Infrequent users can interact confidently, complete tasks faster, and avoid frustration.

  1. Fewer mistakes, fewer tickets, smoother operations

Improved guidance means fewer process errors, fewer escalations, and overall higher data quality.

 

Looking Ahead: Why Joule Will Be a Natural Success in Fieldglass

I am convinced that the combination of real‑world usefulness, intuitive interaction, and rising enterprise trust in AI will make Joule’s adoption in Fieldglass almost automatic.

Organizations that embrace it early will:

 

  1. reduce internal support load,
  2. accelerate task completion,
  3. and empower their users with on‑demand, context‑aware guidance.

 

And with time, as Joule becomes increasingly knowledgeable about Fieldglass configurations, processes, and customer-specific setups, the assistant won’t just answer questions—it will support true automation.

 

Conclusion

Joule is poised to fundamentally change how users engage with SAP Fieldglass—especially those who rarely touch the system.

For PMO teams, administrators, and support organizations, this means less manual work, fewer repetitive questions, and fewer tickets.

For end users, it means greater clarity, faster navigation, and more successful outcomes.

The potential is enormous—and we are only at the beginning.

entry.title: SAP Agent Days Berlin 2026 Recap
entry.content:
At the end of February 2026, SAP hosted its Agent Days event at the SAP office in Berlin - a two-day gathering that brought together SAP product managers, transformation & strategy consultants, and customers from across the Middle and Eastern Europe region. The stated purpose was clear: give attendees a grounded understanding of where SAP stands on agentic AI, what the technology actually consists of, and how companies can begin or accelerate their agent transformation journeys.

This blog post is a condensed summary drawn directly from the session transcripts across both event days. It covers the strategic input, the technical architecture, the development approaches, the customer perspectives, and the practical lessons that emerged so far. For full disclosure: Claude 4.6 Opus Reasoning helped to summarize – however afterwards we had humans in the loop to verify the content and language. 

The event opened with hosts Philipp Sutterlin and Sandra Jörns welcoming the international audience.
